name: Asarone (alpha/beta isomers)
description: Asarone refers primarily to alpha- and beta-isomers found in Acorus species. Current literature emphasizes toxicology, interaction potential, and uncertainty in safe long-term exposure.
effects: Systematic safety reviews describe CNS-active and gastrointestinal effects with dose- and isomer-dependent variability.
Higher beta-asarone exposure is linked to greater toxicologic concern.
contraindications: Avoid in pregnancy and in patients with elevated baseline hepatic risk until safety is clarified.
Avoid prolonged high-dose exposure because toxicology concerns are unresolved.
interactions: Potential interaction risk is reported in safety reviews, but quantitative human interaction data remain limited.
